response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and web software developer with over 15 years of general engineering experience, satisfying the bachelor's-plus-8-years bar on paper. However, the role demands deep specialization in SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his background. His closest relevant work (low-latency DLang services, brief C/C++ IDE component work at National Instruments, a wave-field-synthesis thesis) is peripheral and dated. This is a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers in DLang handling millions of requests/second)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, computer architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance experience
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level silicon or systems performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL or GPU workload expertise
